ads.author: John Phillips
ads.title: Vintage Arcade Machine – Working – Original 1980 Conversion Cabinet
ads.description: Up for sale is a vintage Ms. Pac-Man arcade machine in good working condition. This is an original early-80s unit, not a modern reproduction. This machine started life as a cabinet and was professionally converted to Ms. Pac-Man using a period-correct conversion kit (common practice in arcades at the time). The game runs on an original Pac-Man board with the Ms. Pac-Man daughterboard installed. Details: Fully playable Ms. Pac-Man Original CRT monitor (picture looks good for its age) Classic joystick + buttons Cabinet is solid with normal wear from age Side art present (shows some wear, see photos) Original wiring and internal components largely intact Includes original-style operator settings card inside Condition: Works and plays Some cosmetic wear consistent with age (scratches, fading, etc.) Not fully restored – great candidate for restoration or to enjoy as-is Notes for collectors: This is a Pac-Man to Ms. Pac-Man conversion, not a factory-dedicated Ms. Pac-Man cabinet. These were widely used in arcades in the early 1980s and are part of authentic arcade history. Pickup only – located in New Hope AL. Bring help, this is heavy. Message me with any questions or if you want additional photos or video of it running.

ads.author: David Bolton
ads.title: Pinball machine Hokus Pokus 1975 owned since 1979
ads.description: Edit March 20 We moved into new place and something happened along the way where it is no longer resetting. When you hit the reset button it makes a noise like it's going from one to two player but nothing resets. I know it's something minor. I have lowered the price considerably. Had every switch , blade and contact filed and cleaned and machine shopped out in 2021 by Terri Irwin , a long time respected pinball mechanic in Ocala. Has a few lights out. Only issue is sometimes the sco re reel does not completely set over and you have to hit the button again to restart it. One of those Ghost in the machine kind of things that she just couldn't figure out . I have owned this game since 1979 when I received it as a kid. You might have seen this up for sale in the past and then the listing disappeared. This was a period of time when I needed money for something so I put it up for sale but then something else came through and I took it down because I didn't really want to sell it. At this point in life it is mostly just nostalgia. I just don't play it anymore. It is very fun game I am just bored with it after 45 years and want something different. Very open to trades for a newer pinball machine plus cash on my end. Thanks for looking! Nine zero four four four four one four three nine.. Dave

ads.author: Michael Sedovic
ads.title: Silent Scope 2 Arcade Machine – Fully Working, FRAM Upgrade (No Suicide Chip)
ads.description: Silent Scope 2: Dark Silhouette arcade cabinet in excellent working condition. Everything works exactly as it should. The screen is bright, the scope is accurate, and controls are responsive. Major upgrade, no more failure risk. This machine has been upgraded with FRAM (FM18W08-PG), eliminating the original Timekeeper RAM / “suicide chip” issue that causes many of these to fail over time. This means no battery dependency, no data loss, and long-term reliability. Features: Fully tested and 100% working Original dedicated cabinet (not a conversion) Scope tracks accurately Strong picture and sound Clean and well-maintained This is a plug-and-play arcade classic, ideal for a home game room or collector. Asking $1,400 OBO Located in Buford Pickup only – I can help load These are getting harder to find in fully working condition with this upgrade. Serious inquiries only
